title: Combining a C Routine with Rcpp
text: goal project show hand-written C routine (one talks R R’s C API SEXP type) can called within C++ function , turn, exposed R Rcpp. C code never know Rcpp exists; C++ layer responsible translating R objects C routine. comparison, package also ships pure Rcpp implementation algorithm two approaches can placed side side. example algorithm discrete convolution two numeric vectors b, produces vector length length() + length(b) - 1.
code: . ├── DESCRIPTION                         # Package metadata ├── NAMESPACE                           # Function and dependency registration ├── R                                   # R functions │   ├── cRcpp-package.R                 # Package documentation │   └── RcppExports.R                   # Autogenerated R to C++ bindings by Rcpp ├── README.md ├── man                                 # Package documentation │   ├── convolve_cpp.Rd │   ├── convolve_from_c.Rd │   └── cRcpp-package.Rd └── src                                 # Compiled code     ├── convolve_in_c.c                 # The C routine that uses R's C API     ├── convolve_in_c.h                 # Header exposing the C routine's prototype     ├── convolve_from_c_to_rcpp.cpp     # Rcpp wrapper that calls the C routine     ├── convolve_in_cpp.cpp             # Equivalent algorithm written purely in Rcpp     └── RcppExports.cpp                 # Autogenerated R bindings

previous_headings: Using C Routines inside of Rcpp
title: Combining a C Routine with Rcpp
text: C routine lives src/convolve_in_c.c written directly R’s C API. receives arguments SEXP objects, coerces real (double) vectors, protects garbage collection PROTECT(), allocates output vector length na + nb - 1, fills convolution. matching UNPROTECT() releases protected objects result returned. routine’s prototype declared small header, src/convolve_in_c.h, C source C++ wrapper can see declaration.
code: #include <R.h> #include <Rinternals.h>  #include "convolve_in_c.h"  SEXP convolve_c(SEXP a, SEXP b) {   int na, nb, nab;   double *xa, *xb, *xab;   SEXP ab;   a = PROTECT(coerceVector(a, REALSXP));   b = PROTECT(coerceVector(b, REALSXP));   na = length(a); nb = length(b);   nab = na + nb - 1;   ab = PROTECT(allocVector(REALSXP, nab));   xa = REAL(a); xb = REAL(b); xab = REAL(ab);   for(int i = 0; i < nab; i++)     xab[i] = 0.0;   for(int i = 0; i < na; i++)     for(int j = 0; j < nb; j++)       xab[i + j] += xa[i] * xb[j];   UNPROTECT(3);   return ab; } #ifndef CONVOLVE_C_H #define CONVOLVE_C_H  SEXP convolve_c(SEXP a, SEXP b);  #endif /* CONVOLVE_C_H */

previous_headings: Using C Routines inside of Rcpp
title: Combining a C Routine with Rcpp
text: bridge C routine R supplied src/convolve_from_c_to_rcpp.cpp. routine compiled C, header must included inside extern "C" block C++ compiler uses C linkage mangle convolve_c symbol. exported function convolve_from_c() passes Rcpp::NumericVector arguments straight convolve_c() (convert implicitly SEXP) wraps returned SEXP back Rcpp::NumericVector. // [[Rcpp::export]] attribute tells Rcpp generate R-level binding. exported C++ functions also carry //' roxygen comments (trimmed excerpts brevity); Rcpp::compileAttributes() copies R/RcppExports.R, package’s R help pages generated . R function called directly:
code: #include "Rcpp.h"  // Define the method signature  #ifdef __cplusplus extern "C" { #endif    #include "convolve_in_c.h"  #ifdef __cplusplus } #endif  // [[Rcpp::export]] Rcpp::NumericVector convolve_from_c(const Rcpp::NumericVector& a,                                     const Rcpp::NumericVector& b) {    // Compute the result in _C_   // Import it into R   SEXP ab = convolve_c(a, b);      // Cast as a NumericVector    Rcpp::NumericVector result( ab );      // Return result   return result; } convolve_from_c(1:5, 5:1)

previous_headings: Using C Routines inside of Rcpp
title: Combining a C Routine with Rcpp
text: comparison, src/convolve_in_cpp.cpp implements convolution entirely Rcpp types, without dropping R’s C API. idiomatic Rcpp approach useful contrasting bookkeeping required C routine (manual PROTECT()/UNPROTECT(), SEXP coercion) convenience Rcpp::NumericVector.
code: #include "Rcpp.h"  // [[Rcpp::export]] Rcpp::NumericVector convolve_cpp(const Rcpp::NumericVector& a,                                  const Rcpp::NumericVector& b) {      // Declare loop counters, and vector sizes   int i, j,   na = a.size(), nb = b.size(),   nab = na + nb - 1;      // Create vector filled with 0   Rcpp::NumericVector ab(nab);      // Crux of the algorithm   for(i = 0; i < na; i++) {     for(j = 0; j < nb; j++) {       ab[i + j] += a[i] * b[j];     }   }      // Return result   return ab; } convolve_cpp(1:5, 5:1)
